items.title: CIT CEO misled board over TAFE misconduct investigation, inquiry finds

children.content: An inquiry into the recruitment of CIT chief executive Margot McNeill finds she actively misled the institute's board by denying that there was a finding of misconduct made against her at TAFE New South Wales.

image.altText: A woman with classes in a green suit.

items.title: Canberra's light rail to Woden to take more-direct route after feedback

children.content: The ACT government sought feedback on two possible routes for light rail from the city south to Woden, and is now pushing ahead with its preferred more-direct path.

image.altText: A drawing of the proposed light rail stop outside Albert Hall. The picture shows a tram stopped and cars passing.

items.title: Parents of 14yo athlete who died suddenly work to prevent future heartbreaks

children.content: Two Canberra parents rocked by the sudden death of their teenage son are trying to help him leave a life-saving legacy.

gallery.altText: A couple look solemn while sitting together at a dining table.
A boy wearing a green jumper and smiling.
A boy wearing a white sports uniform kicking a football.

thumbnail.altText: A couple look solemn while sitting together at a dining table.
A boy wearing a green jumper and smiling.
A boy wearing a white sports uniform kicking a football.

items.title: 'Inhumane': Mick's disgust at cops' justification over the tasering of his 95yo mum

children.content: In an in-depth interview ahead of the inquest into his mother Clare Nowland's nursing home death, Mick says the family wants changes that will prevent another tragedy.

image.altText: An older man wearing a short-sleeve black button up shirt stands in his backyard in front of a green fence and hedges.

items.title: A Liberal-Green government. Could it happen in Australia?

children.content: In many ways, the Liberals and the Greens are complete opposites — but in the ACT, an Australian-first power-sharing arrangement between the two parties almost became a reality. So, in politics, can opposites really attract?

image.altText: A graphic of a hand placing a vote with a half blue half green background.

items.title: Motorcyclist's death a reminder to pay attention when driving, loved ones say

children.content: The loved ones of a Canberra motorcyclist killed nearly two years ago say his death is a stark reminder that "full attention" behind the wheel can be the difference between life and death.

gallery.altText: A bald man with glasses and a white beard stands outdoors smiling.

thumbnail.altText: A bald man with glasses and a white beard stands outdoors smiling.

items.title: 'Obvious I lied': Former Canberra doctor faces disciplinary action for lying under oath

children.content: Former Canberra vascular registrar Imran Kader will face disciplinary action after he lied under oath and tried to interfere with a witness during criminal proceedings for an alleged rape.

image.altText: A building sign that reads 'ACT Civil and Administrative Tribunal'.

items.title: Truck driver found guilty of negligent driving over fatal crash in Canberra

children.content: Sean Joshua Walton was driving his truck and turning right at an intersection when he crashed into a black Harley Davidson motorbike being driven by 47-year-old Craig O'Neil.

image.altText: A crumpled motorbike on the ground near a parked truck, at night at an intersection.

items.title: Three die after suspected hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship

children.content: A suspected outbreak of hantavirus, which is spread by contact with rat and mice faeces, has killed three people on a cruise ship.

image.altText: A cruise ship on the open ocean with a smaller dinghy approaching.

items.title: A hacker built a tool to track police officers. Then he tried to warn them

children.content: A security flaw means the location of devices used by police, potentially including undercover officers and those travelling home, is visible to anyone with a phone or laptop.

image.altText: A man wearing a dark hoodie is silhouetted against the lights of a police station with police cars in front.

items.title: Australian businesses falling victim to new type of fraud

children.content: Higher inflation, a war in the Middle East, and a ban on surcharges are crippling small businesses. Now they are falling victim to a modern crime.

articles.title: What would you like to see in the federal budget?

children.content: We want to know what issues matter to you ahead of the federal budget. Have your say.

image.altText: A graphic showing a range of reader comments about potential issues in Australia's 2025 federal election.

articles.title: NAB profit drops as bank warns of Middle East uncertainty

children.content: The ASX has slipped on opening, despite strong corporate earnings propelling the S&P 500 to a new record close. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: NAB bank signage in red, black and white colours

articles.title: Interest rates and recession: What to know before tomorrow's RBA decision

children.content: Business journalist Emily Stewart walks you through the elements that will contribute to the RBA's decision to change the cash rate on Tuesday.

image.altText: Pink and green $100 notes falling

gallery.altText: Bronte looks down at the camera as she paints

items.title: Daughter hopes $1m reward will solve mystery of mother's disappearance

children.content: Marion Barter has not been seen since 1997. Her daughter is determined to find out what happened to her mother, who she believes may have found herself "in a situation she did not fully control".

image.altText: Woman with dark hair poses with two young children by her side

items.title: A new poll tells us a lot about One Nation's new-found support

children.content: A new poll asks Australians specifically about One Nation and the results show that rather than being a flash in the pan, the minor party's freight train may be a more lasting phenomenon.

image.altText: Pauline Hanson smiling at the camera

items.title: Busloads of tourists turn quiet street into 'internet sensation'

children.content: For years, the residents of Tasman Drive in Gerringong knew they lived in a stunningly beautiful part of the world, but then social media discovered it too.

gallery.altText: Aerial photo of a street with ocean views opposite a grassy headland

thumbnail.altText: Aerial photo of a street with ocean views opposite a grassy headland

items.title: Cultural practices harder to do with price of fuel in remote Australia

children.content: Cultural obligations and essential services are being impacted by the cost of fuel in Australia's most remote communities.

image.altText: A woman at the fuel pump

items.title: Chook left in oven, lost remote among 'absurd' reasons for Triple Zero calls

children.content: Triple Zero call centres are seeing an increase in "absurd" calls, ranging from lost television remote controls to ducks crossing the road.

image.altText: Signage for the Australian emergency call number Triple 000 is seen on the side of a police car

items.title: Rice is cheap, easy to cook and the main ingredient in these four meals

children.content: Rice doesn't need to play second fiddle. The cheap and simple pantry staple can hold up a whole meal.

gallery.altText: Parmesan and balsamic risotto served in a green bowl, garnished with herbs.
Vegetarian fried rice that's baked on a tray with eggs, peas, beans, sugar snap peas, leek and asparagus, an easy family dinner.

items.title: It was a record race time. Moments later spectators were suspicious

children.content: Tasmania's top horse race was run 37 metres shorter than advertised. Officials knew the track distance was likely incorrect but proceeded anyway. This is what happened.

image.altText: Elevated view of horse racing event with crowd looking on.

items.title: Tim Roth battles Radha Mitchell in gripping Australian action flick

children.content: Shooting in snake-and-spider-infested grass on a Queensland farm, Seven Snipers spooked its stars.

image.altText: A man in camoflague and a vest hands a bullet to a woman in a wedding dress and red headband.

items.title: China accused of targeting Port Pirie and Hobart metal smelters

children.content: South Australia's treasurer and energy minister says China is undermining Australia's smelting industry and that any shutdown would leave national security "very exposed".

children.content: Take a trip back in time with this week's history quiz.

items.title: 'Motivation to swim faster': Four people bitten by crocodile before swimming race

children.content: Four people were bitten by a suspected freshwater crocodile just a day before a sold-out swimming competition was run in the same far-north Western Australia lake.

image.altText: A freshwater crocodile resting next to a lake

items.title: Traffic gridlock as park visitors turn out to see the turning of the fagus

children.content: Nature lovers who went to see the autumn colours of the fagus at Tasmania's Mt Field National Park on Saturday say there were no car spaces and nowhere to turn around.

children.content: Former New York mayor and US President Donald Trump's former personal lawyer Rudy Giuliani is in hospital in a critical condition.

items.title: We're headed into an AI fog as the automation arms race begins

children.content: Artificial intelligence might be driving growth for data centre companies, but society is unprepared for the long-term risks posed to the rest of us.

items.title: Police search properties 32 years after teenager's disappearance

children.content: Gordana Kotevski was last seen in 1994 when witnesses saw the 16-year-old being forced into a vehicle by at least two people near Newcastle.

items.title: Saifiti medically retires from league due to shoulder issue

children.content: Daniel Saifiti's stellar career with the Dolphins, Newcastle, NSW, and Fiji comes to a premature end due to injury.

image.altText: A Dolphins ball-carrier in the NRL grimaces as he is tackled by two Gold Coast defenders during a game.

items.title: Kasatkina wins first tournament as an Australian citizen

children.content: Fighting back from a set down, Daria Kasatkina wins the Catalonia Open final in Spain.

image.altText: Daria Kasatkina plays a double-fisted backhand at the 2026 Madrid Open.

items.title: Socceroos sweat on mainstay's injury blow ahead of World Cup

children.content: US-based Socceroos midfielder Aiden O'Neill suffers an ankle injury less than six weeks out from the start of the World Cup finals.

items.title: Landlords to lose ability to evict without reason under WA reforms

items.title: Worker shortage sees people struggle in 'most extreme form of homelessness'

children.content: Victoria needs almost five times as many outreach field workers to support people who are sleeping rough, according to a study by the Council to Homeless Persons.

items.title: Russian woman's husband beat her but police were no help, so she wrote to Putin

children.content: Domestic violence and abuse have been described as an "epidemic" in multiple countries, but analysts argue there is something that sets Russia apart: the government is making things worse.
